concepts.title: First-order deformations
concepts.blurb: A first-order deformation of a scheme $X_0$ over a field $k$ is a flat $k[\epsilon]/\epsilon^2$-scheme $X$ with $X \otimes k = X_0$. Geometrically: a map from the dual numbers — the tangent direction in moduli space at $X_0$.

concepts.title: Tangent space of moduli is $H^1(X_0, T_{X_0})$
concepts.blurb: Isomorphism classes of first-order deformations of a smooth $X_0$ form a $k$-vector space identified with $H^1(X_0, T_{X_0})$. This is the tangent space at $X_0$ to the moduli space — when smooth — and gives the dimension count via Riemann–Roch.

concepts.title: Obstructions live in $H^2(X_0, T_{X_0})$
concepts.blurb: The obstruction to lifting a deformation from $\mathrm{Spec}\,k[\epsilon]/\epsilon^2$ to $\mathrm{Spec}\,k[t]/(t^3)$ (and beyond) is a class in $H^2(X_0, T_{X_0})$. Vanishing of $H^2$ implies the deformation functor is unobstructed and the moduli space is smooth at $X_0$.

concepts.title: Deformation functors and Schlessinger's criteria
concepts.blurb: The deformation functor $\mathrm{Def}_{X_0}: \mathrm{Art}_k \to \mathrm{Set}$ assigns to each Artinian local $k$-algebra $A$ the set of flat lifts of $X_0$ to $\mathrm{Spec}\,A$. Schlessinger's criteria characterize when $\mathrm{Def}_{X_0}$ has a hull or is pro-representable.

concepts.title: The cotangent complex $L_{X/Y}$
concepts.blurb: Illusie's cotangent complex $L_{X/Y}$ generalizes the relative cotangent sheaf $\Omega^1_{X/Y}$ to a complex in $D^{\le 0}(X)$. Tangent and obstruction spaces of any deformation problem live in $\mathrm{Ext}^i$ of $L_{X/Y}$ — the unifying object behind the previous concepts.

concepts.title: Worked example: deformations of a smooth curve
concepts.blurb: For a smooth projective curve $C$ of genus $g \ge 2$: $H^1(C, T_C)$ has dimension $3g - 3$ (the dimension of $\mathcal{M}_g$ at $C$), and $H^2(C, T_C) = 0$ (curves have no obstructions). The moduli space $\mathcal{M}_g$ is smooth of dimension $3g - 3$.
